params.name: Fabergé Egg
params.description:
On Mar 24, 1885 AD, Fabergé delivered an egg-shaped jewel to the Tsar.
To console the Empress who had come from afar to marry him, Alexander III ordered Fabergé the jeweler to make an egg. This seemingly ordinary egg contained a golden chicken egg, which in turn held a miniature golden hen. Inside the hen's belly were a diamond crown and a mini ruby egg. This masterful design impressed the Empress and made Fabergé a legend in the jewelry industry. From then on, he provided a new egg for the R …
